text: The Detention Facility Officer shall capture Defendant, Incident, and Financial Information.
The Defendant shall swear to and sign the Financial Affidavit.
The Detention Facility Officer shall witness and sign the Financial Affidavit.
The Detention Facility Officer shall schedule an Indigency Hearing.
The Magistrate shall inform the Defendant of the right to request appointment of counsel and determine if the Defendant wants a Court Appointed Attorney.
The Defendant shall sign a Request for Appointed Counsel if indigent status is determined.
The Magistrate shall submit the Request for Appointed Counsel to the Office of Attorney Appointments.
The Defendant shall sign a Waiver of Appointed Counsel if they waive the right to counsel.
The Magistrate shall submit the Waiver of Appointed Counsel to the Office of Attorney Appointments.
The Magistrate shall submit a notice of indigency determination to the Office of Attorney Appointments if the Defendant is determined to be not indigent.
The Office of Attorney Appointments shall process requests for counsel no later than the end of the first working day after receipt.
The system shall notify law enforcement agencies of denied requests for counsel within 24 hours of denial.
The system shall notify the appointed attorney and Defendant of an appointment within 24 hours of the appointment.
The Office of Attorney Appointments shall research Defendant and Incident information in the Court Case Management System.
The Office of Attorney Appointments shall confirm the Defendant has not been released from custody before appointment.
The Office of Attorney Appointments shall confirm the Defendant does not have a Defense Attorney already appointed on a pending case.
The Office of Attorney Appointments shall select the next Defense Attorney in the appropriate appointment rotation list.
The system shall notify the Defendant and Defense Attorney of appointment via facsimile or electronic mail.
The system shall capture the date and type of first contact with the defendant by the appointed attorney.
The Defense Attorney shall notify the Office of Attorney Appointments of the date and time of initial contact within one working day.
The system shall notify the Office of Attorney Appointments when first contact by an appointed attorney is not made within one working day.
The District Attorney shall notify the Office of Attorney Appointments of charges accepted for prosecution.
The Office of Attorney Appointments shall enter the Defense Attorney appointment into the Court Case Management System.
The Court Coordinator shall enter setting information into the Court Case Management System.
The system shall notify the Defendant and Defense Attorney of court settings.
The Defense Attorney shall submit itemized requests for payment for services performed to the Office of Attorney Appointments.
The Judge shall provide a written finding for approved payment amounts that differ from the requested amount.
The Auditor shall remit payment to the Defense Attorney after Commissioners Court approval.
The Office of Attorney Appointments shall add Defense Attorneys to the appropriate rotation list upon approval by Judges.
The system shall produce reports on the number of appointments made by date range and appointment type.
The system shall calculate the average number of hours between arrest and magistration.
The system shall integrate with the District Attorney Electronic Case Filing System.
The system shall integrate with the Court Case Management System.
The system shall comply with the Texas Uniform Electronic Transactions Act (UETA).
The system shall implement the Global Justice XML Data Model (GJXDM) for technical integration.
